channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.K. Live Updates: Starmer Announces Resignation; Burnham Wins Key Endorsement
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer stepped down as leader of the governing Labour Party. Andy Burnham, the party’s most popular politician, said he would seek the prime minister’s job and secured the support of a potential rival.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain outside 10 Downing Street in London on Monday.

item.title: Mideast Live Updates: Vance Points to Progress After First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Iran had agreed to invite U.N. nuclear inspectors back into the country in the first round of negotiations that ended Monday. Iran did not immediately comment.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking at Emmen Military Air Base in Switzerland on Monday.

item.title: Cuba’s Fuel Crisis Brings Schools to a Standstill
item.description: The country’s already-struggling schools are ending the academic year early because of a crippling fuel shortage caused by the U.S. oil blockade.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin and Frances Robles
item.category: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
item.media:credit: Lisette Poole González for The New York Times
item.media:description: Analeidis Arias Matos getting her son, Alejandro, ready for school last month in Santiago, Cuba.

item.title: Thousands Are Fleeing Ukraine’s Donbas Strongholds as Russia Pushes Closer
item.description: While Kyiv’s fortunes have brightened in other ways in the war, Moscow’s forces are raining bombs and drones on “fortress belt” cities like Sloviansk and Kramatorsk.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd and Oleksandr Chubko
item.media:credit: Finbarr O’Reilly for The New York Times
item.media:description: Evacuees from Sloviansk and Kramatorsk, in the Donetsk region of Ukraine, waited to be processed at a transit hub in Lozova, Ukraine, last month.

item.title: Explosion at Qatar Gas Plant Kills at Least 13 and Injures 66
item.description: The blast happened at a key site for natural gas production. The authorities blamed a technical malfunction while operations were being restarted after the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screen grab from a video showing flames at the Ras Laffan industrial zone in Qatar on Sunday.

item.title: Two Boys Arrested After Deadly School Shooting in the Philippines
item.description: Three people were killed and seven wounded after two teenagers opened fire at their school in Tacloban City, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Aie Balagtas See and Jason Gutierrez

item.title: Pakistan Plans to Cut Tax on Menstrual Pads, but Many Say It’s Not Enough
item.description: Activists said more action was needed to solve the larger problem: Only 12 percent of Pakistani women and girls use safe, commercially made menstrual products.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Anisa Shabir
item.media:description: In Pakistan, many women and girls are unable to afford commercially made pads and tampons. UNICEF estimates that taxes account for about 40 percent of the price of the products.

item.title: First Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Ends With High Hopes and Big Challenges
item.description: Mediators reported progress toward reaching a final deal within 60 days. They also said that negotiators had dwelled on issues that were supposed to be settled.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance talking with officials from Pakistan and Qatar at the meeting in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: Does This Radio Host Really Know Everyone in Wales?
item.description: Elis James thinks he has a friend in common with just about everyone else in Wales. Don’t believe him? Give him 60 seconds to prove it.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
item.media:description: Radio host and comedian Elis James has taken the Welsh penchant for chatting and being social to new heights.

item.title: South Korea’s Starbucks Shut Early After ‘Tank Day’ Promotion Debacle
item.description: All Starbucks stores in South Korea shut early on Monday because of a franchise-wide training instituted in the wake of a disastrous marketing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Lee Jiyoun
item.media:description: A Starbucks coffee shop that closed early in Seoul on Monday.

item.title: Abelardo De La Espriella, Trump-Backed Rightist, Headed for Win in Colombia
item.description: A victory for Abelardo De La Espriella, a lawyer with no previous political experience, would be another win for the right in Latin America.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of Mr. De La Espriella in Barranquilla on Sunday.

item.title: Bitter Historical Feud Threatens Ukraine-Poland Alliance
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine signed a decree honoring World War II partisan fighters that has inflamed tensions with Warsaw.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:description: President Karol Nawrocki of Poland and President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine in December in Warsaw.

item.title: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
item.description: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A painting of Iran’s first two supreme leaders in Tehran this month. The second, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran this year.

item.title: Luxury Kushner Project Collides With Albanian Discontent
item.description: Protests in Albania against plans for a luxury tourist site have become a cause célèbre for opponents of President Trump and his family. But the politics are local.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins
item.media:description: Protests in Tirana, Albania’s capital, against the government this month. Some Albanians worried that their cause was being hijacked by foreign activists.

item.title: How Many Prime Ministers Has the UK Had Since Brexit?
item.description: The years since the Brexit vote have been some of the most tumultuous in Britain’s recent political history. Here’s a look at the revolving door in Downing Street.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Steve Parsons; Pier Marco Tacca/Getty Images; Pool photo by Danny Lawson; Leon Neal/Getty Images; Pool photo by Lucy North; Lucy North/Press Association, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Post-Brexit Britain has seen six prime ministers: David Cameron, Theresa May, Boris Johnson, Liz Truss, Rishi Sunak and Keir Starmer.

item.title: The Guardrails Designed to Protect U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to set up a Strait of Hormuz hotline and a “de-confliction cell” for Lebanon, according to mediators from Pakistan and Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Chatham House (The Royal Institute of International Affairs)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan and Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman of Qatar at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: Who Will Replace Keir Starmer as UK Prime Minister? Here’s What Happens Next.
item.description: Keir Starmer, the British prime minister, could be replaced through a full leadership contest. But if the top contender, Andy Burnham, is the only candidate, the process will be much faster.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his wife, Victoria, returning to 10 Downing Street after he announced his resignation on Monday.

item.title: China Tightens Rare-Earth Grip on U.S. Firms, Threatening Trade Clash
item.description: The move targets two U.S. manufacturers at the center of the Trump administration’s effort to rebuild the domestic supply chain for critical magnets.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: MP Materials’ open-pit rare-earth mine in Mountain Pass, Calif., in 2020.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes and Yan Zhuang

item.title: A Loophole Brings Cystic Fibrosis Patients a ‘Miracle Drug’ in Generic Form
item.description: A generic version of a breakthrough cystic fibrosis drug, manufactured in Bangladesh for a fraction of the American price, may give some families around the world an unlikely lifeline.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen
item.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times
item.media:description: Josua Lottering traveled with his mother from South Africa to Bangladesh to buy a life-changing drug for cystic fibrosis.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.description: The prime minMr. Starmer insisted he would fight any leadership challenge,
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

item.title: The Man Trumpifying FIFA
item.description: Gianni Infantino, the FIFA president, has unabashedly courted the U.S. president’s favor.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Gianni Infantino at the White House in 2025.

item.title: Uncertainty Remains Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iranian militaries have offered conflicting accounts over whether the crucial waterway is open.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to be a point of negotiation between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: As Vance Offers Iran ‘Outstretched Hand’ in Talks, Trump Threatens Bombing
item.description: The administration’s split message came as the United States and Iran began a new round of nuclear negotiations in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Steve Witkoff, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance and the Pakistani Army official Asim Munir at Lake Lucerne in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Have Plenty to Negotiate Beyond Nuclear Issues
item.description: Both sides have committed to discussing sanctions relief and the shape of a reconstruction fund, as well as Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking at the Bürgenstock resort, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Netanyahu faces one of the biggest challenges of his career.
item.description: In a ‘plot twist’, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appears to be heading into an election this fall without his main prop: President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel during a news conference in Jerusalem this week.

item.title: Three Men Are Key to Iran’s Approach to U.S. Talks
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader, foreign minister and parliament speaker all play central roles in hammering out an agreement with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, center, Iran’s foreign minister, arriving near Lucerne, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Keir Starmer Reflects on ‘Political Challenges’ as Pressure to Resign Mounts
item.description: An ally of Keir Starmer’s told the BBC on Sunday that Mr. Starmer was “taking the time to think through what the political realities are today compared to last week.”
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain, second from right, on Friday in London.

item.title: Israel Directs Its Military to Limit Its Actions in Lebanon, but Tensions Persist
item.description: It was not clear whether the new directive would resolve the friction that led to deadly clashes on Friday and Saturday and threatened to derail a preliminary U.S.-Iran peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Christina Goldbaum
item.media:description: Damage from Israeli strike in Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Nuclear Talks Expected to Hinge on These 4 Questions
item.description: Tehran insists that its nuclear program is for peaceful purposes. The Trump administration is demanding assurances that Iran cannot secretly develop a weapon.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, on Sunday at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland.

item.title: A Glamorous Swiss Resort Hosts Officials for U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: The Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people for more than 100 years.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Bürgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne in Switzerland has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people over nearly 150 years.

item.title: While the World Scrambles for Oil, China Sits on Full Tanks
item.description: The possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz may not prompt China to return quickly to prewar levels of oil purchases from the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: An oil refinery in Nanjing, in China’s eastern Jiangsu province, last month. Chinese companies kept their refineries running throughout the war.

item.title: Colombia’s High-Stakes Election: What to Know About the Candidates, Issues and Expected Results
item.description: Colombia’s election on Sunday pits a right-wing candidate endorsed by President Trump against a candidate promising continuity with the country’s departing leftist president.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Joaquin Sarmiento Juan Barreto/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Iván Cepeda, left, and Abelardo De La Espriella will face off on Sunday in a presidential runoff election in Colombia.

item.title: Father and Uncle of Slain New York Girl Given Life Sentences in Pakistan
item.description: The killing of the victim, a 14-year-old girl from Yonkers who was known for posting videos on TikTok, was premeditated, a court ruled.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:credit: Saadullah Akhter for The New York Times
item.media:description: The path outside this family home in Quetta, Pakistan, was where Hira Anwar, 14, was killed.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Ephrat Livni, Aurelien Breeden, Isabel Kershner and Yan Zhuang

item.title: The Women Running Businesses Under Taliban Rules
item.description: With secondary education and most jobs out of reach, thousands of Afghan women have turned to entrepreneurship as the only path to make money and maintain a social life.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Kiana Hayeri
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. and Iranian Officials to Meet for Peace Talks in Switzerland
item.description: Amid a volley of threats, the brief talks largely focused on the war in Lebanon, according to Iranian state media.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A plane carrying an Iranian negotiating team landed at Zurich International Airport in Switzerland on Saturday.

item.title: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
item.description: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.category: Society for the Protection of Nature in Lebanon
item.media:description: Mona Khalil in Mansuri, Lebanon, in 2004.

item.title: Man Is Arrested in Scotland After Rampage Leaves 5 Hurt
item.description: An advocacy group identified some of the victims as Muslims. Counterterrorism authorities are leading the investigation, but a motive for the attacks is so far unknown.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.media:credit: Robert Ormerod for The New York Times
item.media:description: The police in Edinburgh received a report of an attack around 8:50 p.m. on Friday in the Sighthill area. Over the next 40 minutes, officers got reports of other attacks, which they believe are linked, in other parts of the city.

item.title: Huge Blaze Ravages Dominican Republic Beach Resort
item.description: The fire on Friday forced the emergency evacuation of nearly 1,700 guests. Authorities said at least one person, an Italian tourist, was killed.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: A screen shot showing the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel on fire in Bayahibe, Dominican Republic, on Friday.

item.title: Wife of Spanish Prime Minister to Stand Trial for Corruption
item.description: Begoña Gómez faces charges of embezzlement, corruption, misappropriation of funds and influence peddling for helping favored companies win public contracts.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and José Bautista
item.media:description: Begoña Gomez and her husband Pedro Sanchez, the Prime Minister of Spain, at an event in Barcelona this month.

item.title: Canada’s postcard moment at the World Cup is more than the team’s victory.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Nathan Saliba, a player with Canada’s team who replaced the midfielder Ismaël Koné after his injury, held up a team jersey with his teammate’s surname and number, after scoring a goal.

item.title: New Strait of Hormuz Closure Announcement Threatens the Slow Uptick in Traffic
item.description: Fifty-five ships had passed through the strait on Saturday, the U.S. military said. But then Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway once again.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Are Still Fighting, Several Cease-Fires Later
item.description: For the second time in two days, Lebanon was again at the center of whether the fragile U.S.-Iran deal would hold.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Qannarit village, southern Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Shipping remains far below prewar levels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description: A preliminary U.S.-Iran deal took steps to reopen the vital waterway, but traffic remains erratic.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: After Andy Burnham’s Win, the UK Is Set to Test How Far Charisma Can Shift Electoral Dynamics
item.description: The governing Labour Party may oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer in favor of the more popular Andy Burnham — who will still face all the same challenges.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Andy Burnham speaking after winning a special election as a member of Parliament for Makerfield, in northwest England, on Friday. He received almost 55 percent of the vote, easily besting the candidate from Reform U.K.

item.title: Game Change
item.description: New York City’s temporary transformation by the Knicks’ resurgence offers a glimpse of a kinder, more connected world.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: A Court Case Reveals Some of Tim Hortons’ Secrets
item.description: The doughnut and coffee chain has an elaborate system for raising prices and making sure loyal customers pay for their rewards.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: A group of Tim Hortons restaurant owners in Quebec, which does not include the owners of this store in Gatineau, claim that the company has cheated them out of millions of dollars.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Alan Yuhas

item.title: World Leaders Awaited Iran Deal for Months. Here’s Why They’re Still Wary.
item.description: President Trump’s interim agreement with Iran raises hopes for the calming of the world economy. But it leaves many details for future negotiations, putting the world on edge.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: In Tehran this month.

item.title: Abelardo De La Espriella, Right-Wing Outsider, Could Be Colombia’s Next President
item.description: President Trump endorsed Abelardo De La Espriella, the conservative presidential candidate and apparent winner of Sunday’s election who has strong U.S. ties.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal, Patricia Mazzei and Jorge Valencia
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: A campaign rally last month in Medellín, Colombia, for Abelardo De La Espriella, the right-wing presidential candidate.

item.title: Trump Called Iván Cepeda a ‘Radical Left Marxist.’ Can He Be Colombia’s President?
item.description: Iván Cepeda was caught off guard by a right-wing outsider and anti-leftist anger. He tells The Times he is not giving up.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Nathalia Angarita for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iván Cepeda’s campaign jumped into high gear after a right-wing outsider won the most votes during the first round of voting.

item.title: Cape Verde Will Count on Vozinha Against Uruguay at the World Cup
item.description: Cape Verde’s veteran goalkeeper, Vozinha, is keeping the archipelago nation’s hopes outsized after his performance against soccer giant Spain.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Maclean, Andre Amaral and Saikou Jammeh
item.media:description: Cape Verde’s veteran goalkeeper, Josimar José Évora Dias, after the team played to a scoreless tie with Spain on Monday.

item.title: A 175-Year-Old Shipwreck, Lots of Booze and a Korean Island’s Quest to Survive
item.description: A South Korean island is reviving a long‑forgotten episode involving a French whaler as a wine festival, in an effort to stay relevant.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun and Woohae Cho

item.title: Charles Kushner, U.S. Ambassador to France, Riles Paris With Trump Diplomacy
item.description: The U.S. envoy to Paris has emerged as the embodiment of combative diplomacy in the age of President Trump, riling the French establishment notably with his accusations of antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.media:description: Charles Kushner, the U.S. ambassador to France, greeting President Trump on Monday at the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France.

item.title: Lebanon Emerges as Weak Link in U.S.-Iran Deal to End War
item.description: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah, once seen as a secondary front to the American-Israeli war on Iran, has become one of the main obstacles to ending it.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Outside the grave of the former Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah, who was killed in an Israeli strike, in Beirut on Tuesday.
